checks.finding: All in-scope requirements are concretely observable: REQ-001 names verbatim source list/marker text and exact table columns testable via document-conformance TEST-001..004; REQ-002's fail-soft branch (b) is now measurable via TEST-006's three named fixture scenarios asserting exit-0 + comment-call + zero-create-call; REQ-003 is measurable via TEST-013's byte-for-byte v1 hash comparison and TEST-014's existing-suite green re-run; REQ-004/REQ-005 are testable via document-conformance and existing-suite checks (TEST-015..019).

Every goal (REQ-001..REQ-005) has at least one matching AC: REQ-001->AC-001..004, REQ-002->AC-005..011+AC-020+AC-021, REQ-003->AC-012..014, REQ-004->AC-015..017, REQ-005->AC-018..019; acceptance-tests.md's Requirement column confirms identical mapping with no orphaned goal.

Every AC names a concrete externally observable target (file path, table column, exit code, gh-wrapper call count, hash comparison). Round-3's rewritten AC-006/TEST-006 pair now specifies three named fixture scenarios each with an explicit exit-code + call-count + 'no partial-data issue filed' assertion, resolving round-2's ambiguity about asymmetric vendor-failure observability.

Non-goals lists seven explicit exclusions (C1 schema authorship, direct registry writes by the script, precise page-parsing, .ps1 twin of check-model-freshness.sh itself, extending unrelated existing suites, a new WFI template artifact, editing self-improvement.yml), each with a stated rationale bounding the feature's edges.

Security Boundaries table (B1-B4) states trust-boundary/auth-scope/data-classification constraints for fetch-input handling, registry write-prohibition, protected-file staging, and fixture isolation; Roles and Permissions states the human-approval boundary for the protected .github/workflows/test.yml line (AC-011); v1 registry immutability is an explicit migration constraint (AC-013).

All four listed Risks (High/Medium/Medium/Low) carry a concrete mitigation with a validation surface: High-risk External Blocker is enforced as a Main-Workflows task-start precondition; Medium heuristic-imprecision risk is bounded by AC-007's human-triage-on-filed-issue flow; Medium protected-file-miss risk is verified by AC-011's grep self-check; Low risk is a recorded, reviewable design trade-off.
